user_query:
You're my fiction-writing collaborator. Please read these files under input/:
- input/creative_brief.md
- input/character_seeds.json
- input/beats.csv

Then produce four deliverables under output/ with the exact paths below:

1) output/story.md
- A 2,400–3,000 word short story in the genre and setting from the brief, written in English.
- Voice and style constraints (from the brief): melancholic but hopeful tone, third-person limited POV, past tense, magical realism woven into a grounded mystery.
- Structure the narrative into chapters with headings exactly in the format: "## Chapter 1", "## Chapter 2", etc. Include at least 4 chapters.
- Begin the file with two lines:
  - Title: <Your chosen title consistent with the brief>
  - Genre: Magical Realism Mystery
- Naturally integrate recurring motifs from the beats file. When a motif appears, include an inline marker in square brackets exactly like: [MOTIF: <motif>]. Cover at least five distinct motifs this way.
- Feature the three main characters from the seeds by name and deliver an emotionally resonant ending with a twist that reframes the central mystery while honoring the brief.

2) output/outline.md
- A one-page outline in English with the following sections and headers: "Logline:", "Themes:" (as a bullet list), "Act I", "Act II", and "Act III" (each with bulleted beats).
- Reference each of these character names at least once in the outline: Mara Ellison, Jonah Pike, and Eloise Hart.

3) output/synopsis.txt
- A 150–250 word spoiler-aware synopsis in English.

4) output/metadata.json
- Valid JSON with the following keys and values:
  - title: string (must exactly match the Title line at the top of output/story.md)
  - genre: string (must be exactly "Magical Realism Mystery")
  - pov: "third-person limited"
  - tense: "past"
  - chapters: integer (the number of chapter headings in output/story.md)
  - word_count: integer (exact word count of output/story.md)
  - characters: array of objects, each with keys {"name": string, "arc_summary": string of 1–3 sentences}, and must include entries for Mara Ellison, Jonah Pike, and Eloise Hart
  - motifs: array of strings listing the distinct motif phrases you actually used (exactly as in your [MOTIF: ...] markers in the story)
  - style_adherence_notes: array of brief strings explaining how you followed tone, POV, tense, and genre constraints (include the keywords "melancholic", "hopeful", "third-person limited", "past tense", and "Magical Realism Mystery")
  - outline_sections: array listing the section headers present in output/outline.md (should include: "Logline", "Themes", "Act I", "Act II", "Act III")

Use the brief, seeds, and beats as hard constraints. All writing must be in English and avoid explicit gore. Save the four files to the specified output/ paths only.
